UPDATE: I finally was able to get a hold of the attorneys and share my experience. They basically didn’t believe me and sided with the incredibly rude and unprofessional receptionist! Unbelievable, avoid.

Figured I’d share this here for future reference if anyone gets a ticket. I received a minor speeding ticket recently and contacted Fred Hopkins, whom I’ve used in the past.

Experience:
•	Emailed his office 3 times over 2 weeks asking if he could help with copy of the ticket (used email on his site).
•	Never heard back. Sent ticket in requesting a “contest” hearing.
•	Finally called and asked about the ticket. Front desk person Christina was short, rude.
•	I asked about my emails, she said he’s old school, doesn’t use email. I asked if she receives those emails, she said no.
•	She said they would now charge me $50 (“expediting fee”) because the dispute case was in 2 weeks.
•	I asked if she could waive the fee based on me attempting contact 3 different times and having used him in the past, she said no.
•	I asked if I could speak with Fred Hopkins, she ignored me. She told me to go back to ARAG.
•	I told I her I wasn’t really happy with this experience and she essentially hung up on me.

I looked at some online reviews and the say he essentially uses a “deferral”, which you can use yourself if available. I researched what he did for me, turns out he simply used a deferral.

My advice would be to AVOID this law office. This person was the most unprofessional, unpleasant person I’ve spoken to in a long time.
I see no way to actually reach Fred Hopkins directly at this point either to make him aware of his assistant’s behavior.

Two weeks ago, I wrote a detailed email for consultation to this company (as an ARAG member, with a confirmed case number). What did they do? Nothing, nada.

Very professional :(
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Elias, I'm so sorry to hear you didn't get a response. Can you confirm what email you sent that to so we can follow up? We don't have your name in our system anywhere. Thank you!

reviews.text: I'm so glad we turned to Diora for help with our immigration matter. We had a family reunification case that we'd already started independently, but circumstances changed, and Diora guided us through it very professionally. She initially gave us very detailed instructions on how to do everything ourselves, but we decided we needed her support throughout the process because we were so anxious about the outcome. We are so grateful for her support. Diora confidently handled my case and my husband's separately to minimize risks, handled all communications with USCIS, and quickly answered all, even the most silly, questions from anxious clients. Looking back, I wouldn't change a thing, and I know what we paid for: relief from anxiety, confidence in success, and the knowledge that everything was going according to plan, even when we moved from state to state during the process. In the end, I received my work permit and almost immediately my green card in North Carolina 11 months after starting the process in Washington state, and my husband received his work permit a year after starting the process in North Carolina and his green card a year after that, now in California. Thank you so much, Diora!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for your kind and thoughtful words. It was truly a privilege to support you and your husband through each step of your journey.

I did not have a great experience with this law firm. Things started off promising with Colleen as the assigned attorney, but she soon brought in a second attorney to “help” with the case. I was explicitly told I wouldn’t be double charged when both were involved, but that wasn’t honored—there were charges for missed meetings requiring redundant communication, and even times when both attorneys billed for overlapping work.

Eventually, Colleen stopped attending most meetings and was unresponsive to 90% of emails, though I was assured she was still observing and up to date on my case. The day before mediation, she informed me she wouldn’t be joining and that the other attorney would handle it instead—so I wouldn’t be double charged. That was the first time I realized Colleen was no longer functioning as the primary attorney, and that double-charging had clearly been considered despite her earlier assurances.

To make matters worse, I later found out the second attorney’s hourly rate was actually higher than Colleen’s, which raised questions about the intent behind this hand-off. On the day of mediation, Colleen unexpectedly showed up anyway, and when I asked if I’d be charged for her time too, she simply said she’d “fall off now.”

I felt my attorneys were completely unprepared for mediation. Despite billing me hours for reviewing documents and emails, they went into the meeting with only the absolute minimum I had communicated. They hadn’t even received or reviewed the list of items the opposing party claimed reimbursement for—many of which I shouldn’t have been liable for at all. When I asked about this, I was told “these mediations don’t follow logic.” If that’s the case, why were we spending so much time and money preparing documentation, hiring an inspector to value the house, and reviewing materials? I ended up having to advocate for myself in that room.

I would not recommend this firm. It felt disjointed, uninvested, and lacking in transparency. I didn’t see evidence of strategic thinking, legal preparedness, or consideration for client costs.

If you’re just looking to get through a lawsuit and don’t mind high fees, lack of communication, or a passive legal team, this might be the firm for you. Otherwise, I suggest finding a firm that actually invests in its clients, builds strong legal arguments, and treats your time and money with more respect.

The two stars are simply for effort and at the end of the day getting through mediation, but more than that expertise and quality matters most to me for services like this.

reviews.text:
I am thoroughly disgusted with Attorney Chi Nguyen’s lack of professionalism and communication.

I found her through Yelp in July 2025 with glowing reviews. After a July 4th consultation, I hired her to handle my parenting plan matter (not my protection order, though she gave advice on that too). What followed was one of the worst professional experiences I’ve ever had.

Here are the main issues:

Never in court – She never once appeared in person, only via Zoom. We paid her the same as anyone else, yet she didn’t show up physically.

Unprofessional appearance – On Zoom, she dressed in casual T-shirts, not courtroom-appropriate attire.

Paralegal overseas – The paralegal assigned to our case (Bernard) was working permanently out of the country. This made direct communication about our case nearly impossible.

Bad legal advice & dishonesty – She advised my child to drop a protection order to “help” with a 50/50 parenting plan, then denied ever giving that advice in court. This damaged our case.

Unprepared in court – Judge was visibly frustrated with her lack of preparation and awareness of required next steps.

Communication failures – She ignored the client portal (where we receive notifications) and instead sent critical updates by regular email, which caused us to miss deadlines and hearings. Documents were submitted incorrectly and late, resulting in continuances, sanctions, and extra costs we had to pay ($700).

Recent failure – For a GAL hearing, financial documents we sent in late August were submitted at the last minute. She failed to consult us on which GAL we preferred, leaving us blindsided in court.

We left court feeling defeated every single time. Yesterday’s hearing was the final straw—we fired her immediately.

⚠️ Hiring an attorney is stressful enough; you need someone professional, prepared, and communicative. Chi Nguyen was none of these. Do not hire her.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Hanson,

Ryan is an effective injury attorney that did a great job with my case.  My car was hit from behind and there didn’t appear to be much damage. Ryan also helped me find a good and honest doctor to treat my injuries.  Rather than deal with low ball offers Ryan filed suit against the at fault party and got the case resolved on great terms. If you've been in a car crash and need help navigating all the issues, give Ryan a call.  He's a great attorney.

Ryan helped me learn about what insurance benefits I had and then helped me recover those benefits for my injury.  Here’s what happened.  I was in a wreck with a hit and run driver a few years back.   My insurance company paid for some of my medical bills but when my PIP benefits ran out it said there was no money left to pay for treatment or anything else.   They also didn’t tell me anything about the insurance coverage I had paid for to cover me when an uninsured motorist injures me.  Fast forward about 5 years.  I meet Ryan in a social setting and I learn about what he does.   I explain what happened to me and that I wish the uninsured driver who hit me had insurance to compensate me for everything I went through.  Ryan then explained what uninsured motorist (UM or UIM) coverage was and how I had six years to bring a claim under that policy.  I sent Ryan my insurance info and turns out I had the benefits.   My own insurance company didn’t even tell me this!   Ryan then worked tirelessly on my case to help me recover under the policy.  He even tracked down a treatment provider out of state to get some critical medical records that were needed to make my claim.  In the end Ryan really helped me out.  I encourage you to contact him if you need help dealing with a car wreck or your own insurance company.

I was in a car accident during a very difficult time in my life.  I did not have the energy to deal with an insurance company but I wanted to make sure I was treated fairly.   A few months after my collision I hired Ryan to represent me in my personal injury claim.  Before I hired him he answered all of my questions and met me at a time and place which was convenient with my schedule.  As soon as I hired Ryan he got to work on my case.  Unfortunately he discovered that the person who hit me had minimum insurance coverage which wasn’t enough to compensate me for my injuries.  After presenting this information to me Ryan decided that I should file a claim with my own insurance company for the remaining amount which the at-fault driver couldn’t pay.  I followed Ryan’s advice and he did an excellent job working with my own insurance company, and my doctor, to document my injuries and how those affected my life.  In the end we were able to reach a favorable settlement which was a victory!  If you’ve been in a car accident and want to be treated fairly, I suggest you contact Ryan.  He is an excellent injury attorney!

I hired Ryan after I was hit by a car while walking in a crosswalk. Ryan was really easy to work with. When I finished treatment Ryan called my doctor and figured out what type of future care I needed to manage my injury. He used this information to resolve my case for the maximum benefits under the at-fault party’s insurance policy. I was very happy with the results and how responsive Ryan was in answering my questions and making sure my bills were paid. I recommend you call Ryan if you’ve been in a collision. He’s great to work with.

I was injured in a car crash in Everett when another car ran a red light and hit my car. My treatment took a long time and involved several different doctors.  Ryan would constantly check in during my treatment and help me located treatment providers in my area.  Eventually I underwent a very helpful procedure which helped with my pain from the collision and the condition that was exasperated.   When it was time to move forward with my case Ryan hired a specialized medical doctor to meet with me, go over my symptoms, treatments and ongoing injuries.  The doctor wrote a report on my injuries which Ryan used to settle my case for an amount which I was happy with and likely much more than I would have known my case was worth.  I worked with Ryan for three years and I appreciated his thoroughness, communication, listening and negotiation skills which I observed during this time.  If you are in need of a personal injury attorney I highly recommend you contact him.

reviews.text: I had called Moran Kelley PLLC on referral of my financial consultant to obtain information in considering an upcoming need for a Will and Trust. I was fortunate in getting to talk with Amin, one of their legal assistants. At the time I was distraught over possible legal issues with one of my animals, and Amin sensed my distraction so I shared my concerns with her. She immediately related with me, being an animal lover herself who was having health issues with her own beloved pet, and  she chose to calm and comfort my distress first and foremost. Amin put me at ease by explaining how my worry could conceivably work itself out, which it eventually did. I was then able to absorb the information she gave me to think over and follow up on when I was ready. I later met with Susan Kelley and Amin to initiate Moran Kelley's services for my Will and Trust through their expert guidance, and again, both ladies foremost concerns for ensuring my families unique needs shone forth. They offer their knowledge and expertise with compassion and heartwarming, personable care.  I feel and know they will be there to meet any future needs that may arise for me with an expertise that is heart driven.

reviews.text: After suffering a serious traffic accident, I reached out to many personal injury attorneys in the area and was unsuccessful in convincing them that my situation warranted a personal injury case. The reasons cited were many, most notably that I had not severed a limb or broken a spine, or better still, that the amount that can be recovered for lack of grievous near mortal injuries would not be enough to pay for their fees. I got in touch with Stacey through ARAG and she took up my case for what it was worth. Over many months, I saw how she and her assistant, Amin, battled through various obstacles to help me get reimbursed for vehicle damages and most importantly the reimbursement for medical expenses and trauma resulting from the accident. During this time, I was faced with a financial emergency and I remember Stacey and Amin holding off on their professional charges to help me tide over the situation. I decided to retain Stacey's firm as my attorney for any future contingencies. I admire their professionalism, work ethics, patience and a relentless strive to get it done to help the client.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Sunny for your kind review.  It was our pleasure helping you and continuing to work with you.
